articles.title: Signs of ‘feeding’ ritual at dingo burial site shed new light on bond between First Nations people and canines
articles.description: Never documented archaeologically before, evidence points to First Nations people caring for and nursing the animal Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The discovery of a millennium-old dingo burial site in western New South Wales, including evidence of a “feeding” ritual never before documented archaeologically, has shed new light on the longstanding relationship between the canines and First Nations people. The dingo was buried along the Baaka, or Darling River, in Kinchega national park near the Menindee Lakes. Continue reading...

articles.title: Cool Baguio feels the heat: Index nears record at 28°C on May 14
articles.description: BAGUIO CITY — While much of Northern Luzon continues to experience dangerous heat index levels reaching up to 46 degrees Celsius, even the typically cool Baguio City neared its historical peak, with a 28 degrees Celsius on May 14. Weekly data from the Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services Administration (Pagasa) released on Monday covering May 13 to 17, showed Tuguegarao City again recording the highest heat index in Northern Luzon at 46°C on May 15. The same level was also logged on May 12 and May 14. READ: HEAT INDEX WATCH: Danger levels expected in Metro Manila, 36 others [...]... articles.title: ITCZ to bring rains over Southern Mindanao on Monday – Pagasa
articles.description: MANILA, Philippines — The Intertropical Convergence Zone (ITCZ) is expected to bring cloudy skies and scattered rains over southern Mindanao on Monday, May 18, according to the state weather bureau. The ITCZ, according to the Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services Administration (Pagasa), refers to the cloud clusters that make up a line where winds from the northern and southern hemispheres meet.
